skill.name: image-edit
skill.description: Edit images on RunComfy — this skill is a smart router that matches the user's intent to the right edit model in the RunComfy catalog. Picks Nano Banana Edit (batch up to 20, identity-preserving default), OpenAI GPT Image 2 Edit (multilingual in-image text rewrite, multi-ref composition, layout precision), Flux Kontext Pro (single-ref high-fidelity local edit), or Z-Image Turbo Inpaint (mask-driven precise region edit). Bundles each model's documented prompting patterns so the skill gets sharper edits without burning iterations on the wrong model. Calls `runcomfy run <vendor>/<model>/edit` through the local RunComfy CLI. Triggers on "image edit", "edit image", "image-to-image", "i2i", "swap background", "remove object", "rewrite headline", or any explicit ask to edit a single or batch of images.
skill.summary: Intent-routed image editing across four RunComfy models for batch edits, multilingual text rewrites, precise local edits, and mask-driven inpainting.
skill.author: runcomfy-com

security_audit.summary: All 131 static findings are false positives after contextual review. The 94 'external_commands' detections are markdown code blocks documenting `runcomfy run` CLI invocations, not executable code. The 20 'network' findings are documentation URLs and placeholder image URLs (`https://...`) in examples, not actual network calls. The 14 'filesystem' findings reference `--output-dir <absolute/path>` placeholders and `~/.config/runcomfy/token.json` (stored with mode 0600, a security best practice). The 3 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are false matches on the 'MIT' license string and the inpainting 'strength' parameter. The skill author includes an explicit Security & Privacy section documenting token storage, input boundaries, and outbound endpoints.

content.user_title: Route any image edit to the right model on RunComfy
content.value_statement: Image editing needs differ: batch consistency, multilingual text rewrites, single-shot local changes, and mask-driven inpainting each require a different model. This skill reads the user's intent and routes to the correct RunComfy model in one step.
actual_capabilities: Routes user intent to one of four RunComfy edit models: Nano Banana Edit, GPT Image 2 Edit, Flux Kontext Pro, or Z-Image Turbo Inpaint
Supports batch editing of 1–20 images with locked aspect ratio and resolution for series consistency
Handles multilingual in-image text rewrites including Japanese kana, Cyrillic, and Arabic right-to-left scripts
Performs multi-reference composition by combining subject, scene, and palette from separate source images
Executes mask-driven inpainting with tunable strength (0.3–1.0) for retouching, object removal, or full region replacement
Invokes the RunComfy CLI with structured JSON input and writes generated outputs to a user-specified directory
limitations: Picks one model per call; does not blend or chain multiple models in a single invocation
Nano Banana Edit supports 1–20 input images and 1–4 outputs per call; larger batches require multiple invocations
GPT Image 2 Edit accepts only four fixed output sizes (1024×1024, 1024×1536, 1536×1024, or auto)
Flux Kontext Pro accepts a single source image only; multi-image flows must use Route 1 (Nano Banana Edit)

use_cases.title: E-commerce product gallery batch editing
use_cases.description: Apply the same background change or watermark removal across 10–20 product images while locking aspect ratio and resolution for visual consistency.

use_cases.title: Multilingual marketing asset localization
use_cases.description: Rewrite in-image headlines in Japanese, Cyrillic, or Arabic while preserving brand mark, layout, and product identity across language variants.

use_cases.title: Precise photo retouching and object removal
use_cases.description: Remove cables, watermarks, or distractions from photos using a grayscale mask, or make targeted local edits like adding an object to a portrait.

prompt_templates.title: Batch background swap with identity preservation
prompt_templates.prompt: Edit the following 10 product photos: keep the subject identity, pose, and product details unchanged. Replace the background with a clean white studio backdrop. Lock aspect ratio to 1:1 and resolution to 1K for all images.

prompt_templates.title: Multilingual headline rewrite
prompt_templates.prompt: Keep the photograph, layout, and brand mark exactly as in the input. Replace only the in-image headline with the text '今日のおすすめ' in bold Japanese kana, same position and font weight.

prompt_templates.title: Mask-driven object removal
prompt_templates.prompt: Remove the overhead cables using the provided mask. Preserve the rooflines and sky gradient. Apply a slight blur on the mask edges for a clean blend. Set strength to 0.5.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-reference composition
prompt_templates.prompt: Compose the subject from image 1 into the room from image 2. Match the lighting and color palette of image 2. Keep the image 1 subject identity unchanged. Use size auto to preserve input ratio.

output_examples.input: User: 'I need to remove the watermark from these 15 product images and make the background pure white.'
output: Skill identifies batch intent and selects Nano Banana Edit route
Locks aspect_ratio to 1:1 and resolution to 1K across all 15 images
Invokes runcomfy run google/nano-banana-2/edit with image_urls array and preservation-first prompt
Outputs 15 edited images written to the user-specified output directory

output_examples.input: User: 'Change the headline on this poster to Arabic, keep everything else the same.'
output: Skill identifies multilingual text rewrite intent and selects GPT Image 2 Edit route
Constructs prompt specifying Arabic right-to-left script and preservation of layout and brand mark
Invokes runcomfy run openai/gpt-image-2/edit with single image input
Returns the edited poster with the new Arabic headline in place

output_examples.input: User: 'Remove the power lines from this street photo.'
output: Skill identifies mask-driven removal intent and selects Z-Image Turbo Inpaint route
Prompts user for a grayscale mask URL or notes the mask requirement
Invokes runcomfy run tongyi-mai/z-image/turbo/inpainting with strength 0.5
Outputs the cleaned photo with cables removed and sky gradient preserved

best_practices: Lead every prompt with preservation goals before stating the change. For example: 'Keep identity and pose unchanged. Replace the background with...'
Lock aspect_ratio and resolution when processing batches to ensure visual consistency across all outputs.
Use directional spatial language like 'bottom-right corner' or 'upper-right quadrant' to guide the model to the correct region.
anti_patterns: Do not chain multiple model routes in a single call. This skill picks one model per invocation; use sequential passes for compound edits.
Do not pass user prompt content through shell interpolation. The CLI transmits the JSON body directly to the Model API; never construct commands via string concatenation.
Do not skip the mask URL for inpainting tasks. Z-Image Turbo Inpaint requires a grayscale mask; without it the request will fail.

faq.question: Which model does the skill pick by default?
faq.answer: Nano Banana Edit is the default for general edits and batch processing because it supports both single and multi-image inputs (1–20 images) with strong identity preservation.

faq.question: Can I edit more than 20 images at once?
faq.answer: Nano Banana Edit accepts up to 20 images per call. For larger sets, split the batch into multiple invocations of 20 or fewer images each.

faq.question: Is a mask required for every edit?
faq.answer: No. Only Route 4 (Z-Image Turbo Inpaint) requires a grayscale mask. Nano Banana Edit, GPT Image 2 Edit, and Flux Kontext Pro use prompt-based spatial instructions instead.

faq.question: How is my API token stored?
faq.answer: The runcomfy login command writes the token to ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json with file mode 0600 (owner-only read/write). In CI or containers, set the RUNCOMFY_TOKEN environment variable instead.

faq.question: Does the skill send data to external endpoints?
faq.answer: The skill invokes the local RunComfy CLI, which communicates only with model-api.runcomfy.net for request submission and *.runcomfy.net or *.runcomfy.com for downloading generated outputs. There is no telemetry or callback traffic.

faq.question: What happens if I cancel a run?
faq.answer: Pressing Ctrl-C cancels the remote request before the process exits. The CLI does not leave orphaned jobs running on the RunComfy model server.
